message:
[medium] whatsapp: reject base_url userinfo + bad schemes, fix RateLimited regression

Two fixes in fcp-whatsapp:

1. normalize_base_url already guarded query/fragment and pinned the
   host to graph.facebook.com, but missed two gaps:
   - Userinfo (user:pass@...) was accepted. WhatsAppClient builds
     every request URL via `format!("{}/{safe_id}/...", self.base_url)`
     (see client.rs::send_message / update_profile / get_*), so an
     attacker-crafted base_url like
         https://attacker:pw@graph.facebook.com/v21.0
     would bake the userinfo into every outbound request, silently
     overriding the access_token the connector would set via its
     Authorization header.
   - Only `http` was rejected; other non-http(s) schemes like
     `ftp://graph.facebook.com/v21.0` slipped past and were only
     caught by reqwest at request time. Now rejected at config.

2. connector.rs had a pre-existing compile regression: the
   WebhookError::ReplayDetected arm used
       FcpError::RateLimited { violation: Some("duplicate …".into()) }
   but FcpError::RateLimited::violation is `Option<Box<ThrottleViolation>>`
   (structured type), not String — the `.into()` no longer type-checked
   against the current fcp-core signature and fcp-whatsapp would not
   compile from a clean checkout. Webhook replay is not a rate-limit
   event anyway; it's a client error (duplicate event_id), matching
   the InvalidRequest treatment github uses for the same class. Remap
   to FcpError::InvalidRequest with code 1003.

- Five new unit tests on normalize_base_url: accept graph.facebook.com,
  reject userinfo, reject ftp scheme, reject query+fragment, host pin
  still fires for foreign hosts.

Verification: `cargo check -p fcp-whatsapp` under CARGO_TARGET_DIR=
/tmp/fcp-review-p7 is clean (was previously blocked by the
ThrottleViolation regression above). `cargo test -p fcp-whatsapp
--lib` cannot link right now because fcp-testkit transitively depends
on fcp-mesh, and uncommitted parallel-agent WIP in crates/fcp-mesh/
src/{node,symbol_request}.rs leaves a SymbolRequestError::UnauthorizedZone
variant uncovered in an existing match — that breakage is outside
this pane's domain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
